question.text: In the context of PMLA enforcement cases against securities market intermediaries, what is the most common compliance failure identified?

answer.text: Failure to implement adequate AML/KYC procedures and transaction monitoring systems
Failure to achieve minimum annual revenue targets
Failure to list the company's shares on a stock exchange
Failure to obtain ISO certification for quality management

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulatory actions against intermediaries in PMLA cases typically stem from inadequate KYC procedures, failure to maintain proper records, insufficient transaction monitoring, and non-filing or delayed filing of STRs — all of which are AML compliance failures. **The Trap:** Revenue targets and ISO certification are business matters unrelated to AML compliance. The question specifically asks about PMLA enforcement. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XXII (Compliance) tests case-based understanding of regulatory actions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Learn from others' mistakes — study SEBI and FIU-IND enforcement orders to understand common compliance gaps and strengthen your own AML program.
